The '''''FarStar''''' was a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] originally known as the '''''Freedom's Messenger''''' that served as a diplomatic ship for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. After being lost in the [[Battle of Ord Torrenze]], it was recovered by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] and renamed '''''Renegade'''''. After being heavily modified by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the ship was captured by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], renamed ''FarStar'', and used to hunt him down. It was destroyed in the final [[Battle of Kathol System|battle]] against Sarne at [[Kathol (planet)|Kathol]].

==Characteristics==
===Original design===
As a diplomatic ship, the ''Freedom's Messenger'' was equipped with luxurious interior, diplomatic suites, extended droid service bays and entertainment and recreation facilities.<ref name="D1" />

===Wartime refit===
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] the ship was put into combat service. Additional weapon mounts and troop barracks replaced the suites and halls, and the droid bays were used for blaster recharge and maintenance.<ref name="D1" />

===Sarne's modifications===
Sarne had the ship extensively modified to serve as an independent exploration and subjugation ship. The retrofit included a large hangar bay, three extendable docking tubes, advanced turbolaser computers, sensors, and armor. This happened in the hidden shipyards in Korbiss Valley on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] and the use of imprisoned technicians, including New Republic [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Lofryyhn]]. However, when the New Republic attacked Kal'Shebbol in 8 ABY, the refits were still incomplete.<ref name="D1" />

===New Republic modifications===
Moff Sarne escaped the Battle of Kal'Shebbol, but the New Republic task force had to leave immediately for another war theater. To pursue him, the New Republic took over the corvette, since Sarne hadn't been able to destroy it before he left. The ship's sublight and turbolasers were in disarray, and the racks for the TIEs had not yet been installed. This allowed the newly christened ''FarStar'' to carry eight [[T-65B X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65B X-wing starfighters]] and four [[Republic Engineering Corporation|REC]] [[Defender starfighter]]s. It also had two support craft, the ''[[Muvon]]'' and an [[Aegis-class Combat Shuttle|''Aegis''-class combat shuttle]].<ref name="D1" />

==History==
===Old Republic service===
[[File:FarStar_cee_cee.jpg|thumb|left|The ''FarStar'' command center]]
Built by the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] around [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], the ''Freedom's Messenger'' served [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a diplomatic ship.<ref name="D1" />

During its years of service, the ship was at the signing of the Galactic Republic-[[Marzoon Confederation]] treaty as well as delivering a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] delegation to end the [[Vaykaaris Uprising]]. ''Freedom's Messenger'' was widely known as a symbol of peace and hope.<ref name="D1" />

This role changed during the Clone Wars when the ship was refitted to serve as a combat vessel. But despite the different role, the ''Messenger'' served as well as a warship as it did as a diplomatic vessel.<ref name="D1" />

During the [[Battle of Ord Torrenze]], ''Freedom's Messenger'' led an assault force into the blockade to allow for civilian evacuation of the planet. The guns-blazing headlong charge cleared a path for the evacuation ships, but ''Freedom's Messenger'' suffered heavy hull damage and a critical power core breach, delivering a fatal dose of radiation poisoning to its crew. Out of control, the ship finally crashed into the planet.<ref name="D1" />

===Imperial service===
[[File:Steel_Fist_and_FarStar.jpg|thumb|left|The ''FarStar'' in space combat]]
Years later, the corvette was recovered by the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], refitted and recommissioned as ''Renegade'' under the command of Kentor Sarne. Assigned to the [[15th Deep Core Reserve Fleet/Legends|15th Deep Core Reserve Fleet]], the ''Renegade'' fought against [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] as well as several [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] outposts. Later Sarne rose in the Imperial hierarchy, but the ''Renegade'' remained on reserve duty.<ref name="D1" />

In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] a newly appointed Moff Kentor Sarne requested his old ship to be appointed to [[Kathol Sector Imperial Fleet|his fleet]]. Like before, the ''Renegade'' was responsible for several peacekeeping operations, namely the [[Subjugation of Charis]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="D1" />

In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] Moff Sarne had become one of many Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]]. Having also discovered the ancient Darkstryder technology, he took the ''Renegade'' off the active duty list and once again placed it under his personal command. Hidden in his private shipyards in the [[Sorbiss Valley]] on Kal'Shebbol, the ship underwent a massive refit to serve as a long-range exploration and subjugation vessel.<ref name="D1" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] the refit was almost complete, but the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol]] forced Sarne to leave the ship behind and flee to [[Kathol]]. The ship fell into the New Republic hands. Eager to follow the Moff, a hastily assembled crew manned the ship and went into the Kathol Sector.<ref name="D1" />

===New Republic service===
[[File:FarStar_hangar.jpg|thumb|left|Tech crew at work in the hangar bay]]Now christened ''FarStar'', the crew went deep into the Kathol sector and encountered many obstacles on their mission to find Moff Sarne. The crew was forced to abandon ship after suffering severe damage in battle against [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]'s fleet. The ''FarStar'', however, crashed into Sarne's garrison on Kathol, wiping it out.

{{Ship-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
''FarStar'' was also the name of a server—or "Galaxy"—in ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''.
